text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
"Thank you for your track. It was incredible. I'll
handle the mixing myself and contact you again
when the song's complete."

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(I got this DM after I sent over the backing track I
made, but I haven't heard anything since. Maybe
they forgot or they're struggling with it. Or maybe
they gave up...)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(Ugh, it's no good. I can't help thinking about it.
That track could've been better. If I'd used live
instruments, maybe the drop would've—)

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text: Shakur! Are you free on your next day off?

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
If you are, fancy going to a classical concert? It's
an Irish orchestra coming to Japan, and I was
invited to attend. I was wondering if you'd like to
join me.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(An orchestral performance, huh? Can't think of a
better way to clear my head. But...)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
Why're you inviting me to go? There's gotta be
bigger classical music fans out there besides me.

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
That's a fair question. I actually get a lot of
opportunities to listen to classical, and many of
my memories revolve around it, so—

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
Never mind, I'll go. I was leaning towards saying
yes from the start anyway. I don't need to hear
your life story, so save it.

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
Splendid! ♪ We can have some tea afterward as
well!

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: (So, the concert itself was decent, but...)

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
Ah, the first violinist had adjusted the timbre of
their instrument with you in mind.

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
Yes, since long ago, they've always added a
playful edge to their performance when they
know I'm in attendance. It's fascinating how
personality comes through someone's music. ♪

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
You should try listening to the Emperor play
sometime. She follows every notation on the
sheet music to a tee. It's so textbook standard,
she'll put you right to sleep.

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
She must have a full understanding of the
composer's intent, then! I bet your way of playing
is charming in its own right. I'd love to hear you
two perform a duet! ♪

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: (This awkward silence is killing me.)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(Rudolf's here to deepen her understanding of
other countries, and Sirius wanted to see
something new. Ugh, it's a nightmare having these
two together.)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(I should've left before Fine opened her mouth. I
bet they're here because they're sticklers for
etiquette and couldn't turn down her "royal
highness's" invitation.)

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
So, Air Shakur, what was your favorite part of
today's performance?

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(Ugh, don't drag me into this. Damn model
student, being mindful of everyone... This whole
thing's for show anyway, so I'll just say whatever.)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(Wait. How come it's on me to play along with
them? They won't understand my answer either
way, so...)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
The final motif of the fourth movement. I liked
how the violins crescendoed to the verge of being
pure noise.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
But right before they reached their exponential
peak, they worked the room reverb into the
equation and kept things in check. They really
had their numbers down.

text_block_list.name: Sirius & Fine
text_block_list.text:
Absolutely.
Yeah.

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: ...Ngh.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
I concur. I imagine the conductor incorporated a
bevy of accents in order to express how every
moment in that movement felt like an eternity.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
What? No way. You got it backwards. They were
definitely emphasizing how that was the main
movement and were trying to give the
composition a new interpretation.

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
The sound of the violins was so sweet and
beautiful. I was quite impressed by how hard they
worked to keep everything in harmony!

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(Ah, yeah... Music's subjective. Guess it makes
sense their opinions would be so split.)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(This kind of disagreement would never arise in
mathematical formulas with fixed answers. Still,
music lends itself to multiple interpretations. I
don't permit myself that kind of ambiguity.)

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
(If the alternative's regretting I didn't do more, I'd
rather eliminate uncertain factors where possible
and provide ample proof for my answer.)

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: While strolling beside the river...

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text: Fine, you know any other people who play violin?

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
And don't go naming any national talents. I want
someone who plays for fun.

text_block_list.name: Fine Motion
text_block_list.text:
My, what could you possibly be plotting? Well,
my going rate's one bowl of ramen for every note
I play. ♪

text_block_list.name: Air Shakur
text_block_list.text:
Those terms are ridiculous. And I don't want to
hear you play anyway.
